title: Admin Flagged Transaction Review and Action
description: The system shall allow an admin to review flagged suspicious transactions and update their status by choosing between approve, reject, or escalate actions.
rationale: To maintain system integrity and prevent fraud, admins need the capability to respond appropriately to flagged transactions.
business_value: Enhances security and compliance by ensuring that all suspicious activities are properly reviewed and managed in a timely manner, reducing the risk of fraudulent transactions.

source.description: As a admin I need to review flagged suspicious transactions and decide whether to approve, reject, or escalate them So that I can maintain system integrity and prevent fraud

acceptance_criteria: I can see each flagged transaction with its details
The transaction status is updated accordingly
The action is logged for audit purposes
preconditions: I am logged in as an admin
A list of suspicious transactions is available
postconditions: The selected action (approve, reject, or escalate) has been applied to the transaction
The transaction status has been updated in the system
The action has been logged for audit purposes
security_requirements: Access to flagged transactions is restricted to admins with appropriate permissions
All actions on flagged transactions are logged and accessible to authorized users for audit purposes
edge_cases: What happens if an admin tries to approve a transaction that is already in the process of being escalated?
How should the system handle multiple simultaneous actions on the same transaction by different admins?
What if there are no flagged transactions available when the admin logs in?
dependencies: System must have a mechanism for flagging transactions as suspicious.
Admins must have appropriate permissions to view and act on flagged transactions.

interfaces.name: Transaction Review Interface
interfaces.description: The user interface where admins can view flagged transactions and select actions.

interfaces.name: Flagged Transactions API
interfaces.description: API to retrieve flagged transactions and update their status based on admin actions.

test_cases.description: Admin logs in as an authorized user.
test_cases.expected_result: System displays the login screen with correct credentials prompt.

test_cases.description: Admin views a list of flagged transactions.
test_cases.expected_result: A list of flagged transactions is displayed, showing details such as transaction ID, amount, and timestamp.

test_cases.description: Admin selects 'Approve' action on a flagged transaction.
test_cases.expected_result: The transaction status changes to 'Approved', and the action is logged in the audit trail.

test_cases.description: Admin selects 'Reject' action on a flagged transaction.
test_cases.expected_result: The transaction status changes to 'Rejected', and the action is logged in the audit trail.

test_cases.description: Admin selects 'Escalate' action on a flagged transaction.
test_cases.expected_result: The transaction status changes to 'Escalated', and the action is logged in the audit trail.
